---
title: "Introduction: Getting started"
date: "2026-10-06"
author: "Prof. Jane Smith"
organization: "My University"
institute: "Institute of Something"
email: "jane.smith@university.edu"
course: "My Course"
term: "WS 2026"
lang: "en"
format:
  html:
    argdown:
      mode: web-component
      sourceHighlighter: web-component
      config: ../../argdown.config.json
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: a4
    toc: false
    include-in-header: ../../assets/latex/_handout-packages.tex
    template-partials:
      - ../../assets/latex/before-body.tex
    syntax-definitions:
      - ../../assets/pandoc/argdown.xml
    syntax-highlighting: ../../assets/pandoc/argdown.theme
filters:
  - ../../node_modules/.bin/argdown-filter
argdown:
  mode: inline
  format: svg
  config: ../../argdown.config.json
reference-section-title: "References"
bibliography: ../../bibliography.bib
---

## Learning Objectives


- Understand the scope and goals of the course
- Know the assessment format and grading criteria


## Content

### What is Pastafarianism?

The **Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster (FSM)** was founded in 2005 by Bobby Henderson, a physics graduate from Oregon State University. Henderson's founding document was an open letter to the Kansas State Board of Education, which had recently voted to allow *intelligent design* alongside evolutionary theory in public school science classes. In the letter, Henderson demanded equal class time for his own supernatural creation theory: that the universe was created by an omnipotent deity made of spaghetti and meatballs [@cho_tax_2023].

The letter went viral. In 2006, Henderson published *The Gospel of the Flying Spaghetti Monster*, which codified the movement's doctrine. Followers are known as **Pastafarians** — a portmanteau of *pasta* and *Rastafarianism*.

### Core Tenets

| Tenet | Content |
|-------|---------|
| Creator | The FSM created the universe "while quite drunk," touching the Earth with his noodly appendage |
| Scripture | *The Gospel of the Flying Spaghetti Monster* (Henderson, 2006) |
| History | The FSM deliberately hides evidence of creation to test the faith of believers |
| Holy figures | Pirates are the original Pastafarians; the decline of piracy causes global warming |
| Moral code | "The Eight I'd Really Rather You Didn'ts" |
| Afterlife | Heaven, which contains a beer volcano and a stripper factory |
| Ritual dress | A pasta colander worn on the head (used in official ID photos in several countries) |

### Origins and Context

Henderson's protest had an immediate effect: the Kansas Board reversed its ruling in 2006. The open letter, however, took on a life of its own. What began as targeted political argumentation became a movement with genuine adherents across multiple countries.

The question of whether those adherents hold their beliefs *sincerely* — and what follows from that — has become a live legal and philosophical issue. Courts in the United States, Germany, Austria, New Zealand, the Netherlands, and Italy have all considered Pastafarian claims [@palmisano_pastafarian_2025].

### Henderson's Founding Argument

The logical structure of Henderson's letter can be formalised as a premise–conclusion structure:

```argdown
===
sourceHighlighter:
    removeFrontMatter: true
===

[Equal time]: The Flying Spaghetti Monster theory deserves equal time
              in public school science classes.

<Equality argument>

(1) If one supernatural creation theory receives time in public school science classes,
    all other supernatural theories are equally entitled to time.
(2) Intelligent design is a supernatural creation theory that received time
    in Kansas public school science classes.
----
(3) [Equal time]
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Note on premise (1):** Henderson treats this as a conditional applying to *any* supernatural theory. A natural objection is that premise (1) only holds if the theories are sufficiently similar in evidential status. This objection — and others — will be reconstructed in Session 2.
:::

## Exercises

1. **Identify the premises.** State the two premises of Henderson's equality argument in your own words. Are both plausible? Explain briefly.

2. **Challenge a premise.** Which premise is easier to attack? Formulate a one-sentence objection.

3. **Reconstruct in Argdown.** Using the Argdown notation from the session, reconstruct the following objection as a counter-argument to `[Equal time]`:

   > "Intelligent design is not a scientific theory at all. It makes no testable predictions and therefore has no place in a science class, regardless of how many other theories do or do not."

4. **Research question.** Find one example of a Pastafarian claiming a legal right (e.g. to wear a colander in an ID photo) in any country. Briefly describe the outcome.
